questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph : Test (Q). A compound ' X ' was fused with Na metal and the extract gave a white precipitate with $\mathrm{AgNO}_3$. The Lassaigne's extract gave red colouration with neutral $\mathrm{FeCl}_3$. Test $\mathbf{R}$ While compound ' Y ' when fused with Na metal and subsequent analysis on its Lassaigne's extract did not give any characteristic test. Test $\mathbf{S}$ While compound Y on fusion with fusion mixture (sodium carbonate + potassium nitrate) or $\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$, followed by extraction, followed by addition of conc. $\mathrm{HNO}_3$ and ammonium molybdate gave a yellow precipitate.Question : Compound X
questions.solution: Choice (B) is correct because we cannot be definite about halogens, i.e., choice (A) is incorrect because we have not eliminated the presence of CN , for even AgCN is white precipitate hence choice (C) and (D) are incorrect.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph:Metal nitrate $(A)$ on heating decomposes, leaving a solid residue $(B)$ which goes into solution with dilute HCl . The solution of (B) gives a white precipitate (C) with ammonium carbonate solution. The precipitate (C) is dissolved in dilute HCl and the solution is treated with potassium chromate to get yellow precipitate (D). The solution (B) with dilute $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ also gives a white precipitate $(\mathrm{E})$ insoluble in dilute HCl and nitric acid. The precipitate $(\mathrm{E})$ is a part of a white pigment lithopone.Question:The yellow precipitate (D) is :
questions.solution:
Lithopone is a white pigment made from a mixture of ZnS and $\mathrm{BaSO}_4$
$\underset{(\mathrm{A})}{\mathrm{Ba}\left(\mathrm{NO}_3\right)_2} \xrightarrow{\Delta} \underset{(\mathrm{~B})}{\mathrm{BaO}}+2 \mathrm{NO}_2+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_2$
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{BaO}+2 \mathrm{HCl} \rightarrow \mathrm{BaCl}_2+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \\
& \mathrm{BaCl}_2+\left(\mathrm{NH}_4\right)_2 \mathrm{CO}_3 \rightarrow \mathrm{BaCO}_3 \downarrow(\mathrm{C})+2 \mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{Cl} \\
& \mathrm{BaCO}_3+2 \mathrm{HCl} \rightarrow \mathrm{BaCl}_2+\mathrm{CO}_2+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \\
& \mathrm{BaCl}_2+\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{CrO}_4 \rightarrow \mathrm{BaCrO}_4(\mathrm{D})+2 \mathrm{KCl} \\
& \mathrm{BaCl}_2+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4 \rightarrow \mathrm{BaSO}_4+2 \mathrm{HCl}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph:Metal nitrate $(A)$ on heating decomposes, leaving a solid residue $(B)$ which goes into solution with dilute HCl . The solution of (B) gives a white precipitate (C) with ammonium carbonate solution. The precipitate (C) is dissolved in dilute HCl and the solution is treated with potassium chromate to get yellow precipitate (D). The solution (B) with dilute $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ also gives a white precipitate $(\mathrm{E})$ insoluble in dilute HCl and nitric acid. The precipitate $(\mathrm{E})$ is a part of a white pigment lithopone.Question:The compound $(\mathrm{E})$ is :
questions.solution:
Lithopone is a white pigment made from a mixture of ZnS and $\mathrm{BaSO}_4$
$\underset{(\mathrm{A})}{\mathrm{Ba}\left(\mathrm{NO}_3\right)_2} \xrightarrow{\Delta} \underset{(\mathrm{~B})}{\mathrm{BaO}}+2 \mathrm{NO}_2+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_2$
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{BaO}+2 \mathrm{HCl} \rightarrow \mathrm{BaCl}_2+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \\
& \mathrm{BaCl}_2+\left(\mathrm{NH}_4\right)_2 \mathrm{CO}_3 \rightarrow \mathrm{BaCO}_3 \downarrow(\mathrm{C})+2 \mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{Cl} \\
& \mathrm{BaCO}_3+2 \mathrm{HCl} \rightarrow \mathrm{BaCl}_2+\mathrm{CO}_2+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \\
& \mathrm{BaCl}_2+\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{CrO}_4 \rightarrow \mathrm{BaCrO}_4(\mathrm{D})+2 \mathrm{KCl} \\
& \mathrm{BaCl}_2+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4 \rightarrow \mathrm{BaSO}_4+2 \mathrm{HCl}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph : Test (Q). A compound ' X ' was fused with Na metal and the extract gave a white precipitate with $\mathrm{AgNO}_3$. The Lassaigne's extract gave red colouration with neutral $\mathrm{FeCl}_3$. Test $\mathbf{R}$ While compound ' Y ' when fused with Na metal and subsequent analysis on its Lassaigne's extract did not give any characteristic test. Test $\mathbf{S}$ While compound Y on fusion with fusion mixture (sodium carbonate + potassium nitrate) or $\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$, followed by extraction, followed by addition of conc. $\mathrm{HNO}_3$ and ammonium molybdate gave a yellow precipitate.Question : Compound $Y$ contain
questions.solution: Compound Y contains phosphorous as test 's' is confirming this, hence choice (D) is correct while (A), (B) and (C) are incorrect.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
